title: Problem compiling a simple test bench
body:
I'm unable to build a simple testbench that works in Questa.  I'm new to Verilator simulation and am unsure if the problem is in my installation, my Linux environment, or the Verilog code.

Here is the failing file (remove the .txt extension)

[fulladder.sv.txt](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13786456/fulladder.sv.txt)

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
either of the following two
verilator --binary --top-module testbench fulladder.sv
verilator --timescale "1ns/1ns" --timing -cc --exe --build --top-module testbench fulladder.sv

I get a bunch of errors about coroutines.
```
harris@chips:~/cvw/examples/verilog/fulladder$ verilator --timescale "1ns/1ns" --timing -cc --exe --build --top-module testbench fulladder.sv
make: Entering directory '/home/harris/cvw/examples/verilog/fulladder/obj_dir'
ccache g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/usr/local/share/verilator/include -I/usr/local/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-overloaded-virtual -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable        -c -o verilated_timing.o /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp
In file included from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.h:47,
                 from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp:23:
/usr/include/c++/11/coroutine:334:2: error: #error "the coroutine header requires -fcoroutines"
  334 | #error "the coroutine header requires -fcoroutines"
      |  ^~~~~
In file included from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.cpp:23:
/usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_timing.h:100:10: error: ‘coroutine_handle’ in namespace ‘std’ does not name a template type
  100 |     std::coroutine_handle<> m_coro;  // The wrapped coroutine handle
...
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
Verilator 5.019 devel rev v5.018-101-g54ad04b6e

What OS and distribution are you using?
I am running Ubuntu 22.04LTS and updated all of of the dependent tools per the Verilator installation instructions.  I compiled Verilator from source last week.

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I think the question is too basic.
